---
title: "#social-media"
format: gfm
---


## 2022-11-16

*10:54:38 - @Maria Doyle has joined the channel*
**Maria Doyle** (10:54:38):
> set the channel description: Group for Bioconductor social media discussions (Blog, Twitter, Mastodon, LinkedIn, YouTube, Bilibili, WeChat, Weibo etc). Aligned to social media working group.

**Maria Doyle** (11:06:52):
> PR to create new working group for social media is here[https://github.com/Bioconductor/BiocWorkingGroups/pull/19](https://github.com/Bioconductor/BiocWorkingGroups/pull/19). If you'd like to be a member of the working group please give a thumbs up here and if you're interested to co-lead let me know!
  - Attachment: [#19 Add social media working group](https://github.com/Bioconductor/BiocWorkingGroups/pull/19)

**Maria Doyle** (11:09:02) (in thread):
> @Kozo Nishida,@Kevin Rue-AlbrechtI think you and maybe others (but I don't know who) put a hand up to be on this group at last week's CAB meeting?

*11:28:22 - @Laurent Gatto has joined the channel*
*11:34:45 - @Nitesh Turaga has joined the channel*
*12:39:58 - @Lluís Revilla has joined the channel*
*13:05:54 - @Federico Marini has joined the channel*
*18:27:24 - @Leo Lahti has joined the channel*
*23:21:14 - @Xueyi Dong has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-17

*02:37:16 - @Kozo Nishida has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-18

*08:33:13 - @Susan Holmes has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-19

*05:56:20 - @Aedin Culhane has joined the channel*
**Aedin Culhane** (05:57:09):
> @Maria Doylemaybe announce creation of channel in bioc website and general channels


## 2022-11-21

*06:01:24 - @Helen Lindsay has joined the channel*
*06:37:42 - @Lori Shepherd has joined the channel*
*06:37:57 - @Hans-Rudolf Hotz has joined the channel*
*09:37:31 - @Antonio Cembellin Prieto has joined the channel*
*10:56:16 - @Yu Zhang has joined the channel*
*16:40:46 - @Tyrone Chen has joined the channel*
*18:34:32 - @Shila Ghazanfar has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-22

*05:58:40 - @Federica Gazzelloni has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-23

*12:29:35 - @Charlotte Soneson has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-24

*21:05:55 - @Shixiang Wang has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-27

*11:10:28 - @irem Kahveci has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-30

**Maria Doyle** (16:24:35):
> We would love:two_hearts:more members for the Bioconductor social media working group, if you'd be interested to join please let me know.


## 2022-12-06

**Shixiang Wang** (06:52:03) (in thread):
> What should I do for this? I have a WeChat media account could post Bioc updates

**Maria Doyle** (12:05:35):
> <!channel>if you've put your hand up to be on the social media working group, or are interested in finding out more about it, please fill in your availability at this link[http://whenisgood.net/bioc-social-media](http://whenisgood.net/bioc-social-media)***before this Friday 9th Dec***and I will see if we can schedule a meeting before Christmas at whatever time works for most people.

**Maria Doyle** (12:06:42) (in thread):
> Thanks@Shixiang Wang, if you could join this meeting would be great
> > ****@channel**if you've put your hand up to be on the social media working group, or are interested in finding out more about it, please fill in your availability at this link[http://whenisgood.net/bioc-social-media](http://whenisgood.net/bioc-social-media)***before this Friday 9th Dec***and I will see if we can schedule a meeting before Christmas at whatever time works for most people.


## 2022-12-07

**Shixiang Wang** (03:34:27) (in thread):
> Thanks, I am taking a look@Maria Doyleoyle


## 2022-12-08

*09:41:41 - @Johannes Rainer has joined the channel*

## 2022-12-12

*12:39:19 - @Hamdi Furkan Kepenek has joined the channel*

## 2022-12-15

*09:39:38 - @Mercilena Benjamin has joined the channel*

## 2022-12-19

*02:42:56 - @Jeremy Selva has joined the channel*

## 2022-12-27

*05:40:36 - @Michael Love has joined the channel*

## 2023-01-26

**Maria Doyle** (11:26:35):
> <!channel>we're going to have the first meeting of the social media working group next Wednesday Feb 1st 11am UTC
> Agenda is[here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1EU0kinP0sPAVip2AaoNoxVevWUytwRglVeHbEMjbh2o/edit?usp=sharing), please feel free to add comments.
> If you'd like to join the meeting let me know and I can send you the invite and Zoom link.


## 2023-02-01

**Shixiang Wang** (06:01:52) (in thread):
> I am on a high speed railway and sorry for not able to attend the meeting

**Maria Doyle** (07:30:03) (in thread):
> No worries, thanks for letting us know. We've added notes from the meeting to the agenda


## 2023-02-03

*11:29:45 - @Andres Wokaty has joined the channel*
**Andres Wokaty** (11:33:55):
> Hi, I was just wondering if there's someone who approves the PRs for the community blog or if we can merge when ready? The our Outreachy interns made a PR for a really nice post discussing their experience.

**Maria Doyle** (12:56:12) (in thread):
> Yes it's a very nice post:blush:At the moment it's@Kozo Nishidawho can approve & merge

**Kozo Nishida** (13:12:57) (in thread):
> Sorry@Andres Wokaty,  I didn't notice[https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/30](https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/30).:pray:I'll merge the PR right now.
  - Attachment: [#30 Blog: Bioconductor(Outreachy) internship experience](https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/30)
    > [@mblue9](https://github.com/mblue9) [@jwokaty](https://github.com/jwokaty)
    > 
    > Here is the blog Kirabo and I have been working on.

**Kozo Nishida** (13:42:25) (in thread):
> @Andres WokatySorry, I'll reply to your comment in the PR before merging.

**Andres Wokaty** (13:43:41) (in thread):
> Thank you!


## 2023-07-11

*11:51:52 - @Kevin Rue-Albrecht has joined the channel*
**Kevin Rue-Albrecht** (11:53:03):
> Where do we stand on Meta’s Threads? Buzz happening about it scooping loads of users away from Twitter:
  - File (PNG): [IMG_1054](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F05GHHPG4UA/img_1054.png)

**Kevin Rue-Albrecht** (11:54:32):
> The article is making a pretty interesting analysis of how other alternatives (doesn’t mention Mastodon) struggle to capture even niches of users[https://apple.news/A68yTUzZbSf2YRwf2HqyNDQ](https://apple.news/A68yTUzZbSf2YRwf2HqyNDQ)
  - Attachment (apple.news): [Voices: $44 billion and eight months later. It’s finally all over for Elon Musk — The Independent](https://apple.news/A68yTUzZbSf2YRwf2HqyNDQ)
    > It’s all clear in hindsight


## 2023-07-12

**Maria Doyle** (07:35:38) (in thread):
> We discussed it briefly at the Monday’s teaching meeting. It looks interesting but it’s not available in the EU[https://www.euronews.com/next/2023/07/06/why-has-threads-metas-answer-to-twitter-not-launched-in-the-eu](https://www.euronews.com/next/2023/07/06/why-has-threads-metas-answer-to-twitter-not-launched-in-the-eu)
  - Attachment (euronews): [Why is Threads, Meta’s Twitter rival, not launching in the EU?](https://www.euronews.com/next/2023/07/06/why-has-threads-metas-answer-to-twitter-not-launched-in-the-eu)
    > Meta’s new app has been listed in the US and the UK, but has no foreseeable launch date in the EU yet as the company worries about the bloc’s privacy regulations.


## 2023-07-13

**Andres Wokaty** (09:59:11):
> Hi@Kozo Nishidaour most recent batch of Outreachy interns have written a blog post:[https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/48](https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/48). If you don't see any issues, could you merge?
  - Attachment: [#48 Blog: Our Journey as Outreachy Interns with Bioconductor](https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/48)


## 2023-07-14

**Kozo Nishida** (03:13:16) (in thread):
> Yes. I can do the merge.
> Thank you for your contribution:pray:

**Kozo Nishida** (03:41:21) (in thread):
> @Andres WokatyI merged[https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/48](https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/48)and published the contribution post in[https://blog.bioconductor.org/](https://blog.bioconductor.org/). Thank you for your contribution!:+1:
  - Attachment: [#48 Blog: Our Journey as Outreachy Interns with Bioconductor](https://github.com/Bioconductor/biocblog/pull/48)


## 2023-07-27

**Aedin Culhane** (11:30:19) (in thread):
> Also since X…Twitter is depressing.There were far fewer ismb tweets that we might have expected in previous years.No on was live tweeting I think.


## 2023-08-04

*09:36:18 - @Trisha Timpug has joined the channel*

## 2023-08-28

*07:39:35 - @Mikaila Chetty has joined the channel*

## 2023-09-08

**Federico Marini** (05:40:02):
> any plan on joining the new blue sky platform as well?

**Federico Marini** (05:40:17):
> as of now it is invitation only, and I just joined so I cannot pass along invitations

**Federico Marini** (05:42:09):
> more about it ->[https://blueskyweb.xyz/faq](https://blueskyweb.xyz/faq)
  - Attachment (blueskyweb.xyz): [Frequently Asked Questions - Bluesky](https://blueskyweb.xyz/faq)

**Laurent Gatto** (07:00:25):
> This has been discussed briefly, but no clear plans yet.

**Maria Doyle** (13:18:10):
> Yes social media working group have been discussing Bluesky, any feedback on it is very welcome here


## 2023-09-12

**Aedin Culhane** (04:48:37):
> [https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-023-02554-0](https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-023-02554-0)
  - Attachment (Nature): [Thousands of scientists are cutting back on Twitter, seeding angst and uncertainty](https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-023-02554-0)
    > A Nature survey reveals scientists’ reasons for leaving the social-media platform now known as X, and what they are doing to build and maintain a sense of community.

**Aedin Culhane** (04:49:31):
> Roughly 46% have joined other social-media platforms, such as Mastodon, Bluesky, Threads and TikTok.

**Aedin Culhane** (04:52:02):
> In**Nature**’s survey, LinkedIn was the second most popular place for respondents to open new accounts, and Instagram, owned by Meta, was third

**Michael Love** (08:06:17):
> it makes a lot of sense even just as a backup. I’m on Bluesky and Mastodon, which both have their +/- but to me both preferable to X

**Lluís Revilla** (08:17:52):
> I don't know if there are differences between countries but in Spain I've seen some people in Mastodon (not many), but many people in Twitter haven't heard of Bluesky (yet?)

**Laurent Gatto** (11:09:22):
> @Michael Love- you had mentioned that for some underrepresented groups, Twitter was still better than Mastodon, since they had well established communities. Do you know it this still holds with the latest decline of X?

**Michael Love** (11:11:27):
> that’s a good question, I’ve been wondering that myself this past week since joining bluesky

**Michael Love** (11:11:51):
> i think some of the issues raised by individuals from UR communities previously will show bluesky > mastodon

**Michael Love** (11:14:23):
> but this is hard for me to perceive and gauge, i think it will be a wait-and-see situation


## 2023-09-19

**Maria Doyle** (03:30:16):
> :mega:***Bioconductor Community Survey on Twitter/X Use***:mega:Hello everyone! As part of our ongoing discussions about our community’s presence on Twitter/X and other social media platforms, the Bioconductor[social media working group](https://workinggroups.bioconductor.org/currently-active-working-groups-committees.html#social-media)have created a short (< 5 mins) survey to gather your thoughts and preferences. Your feedback is important to shaping the future of how we communicate as a community.:link:Survey link:[https://t.maze.co/187240083](https://t.maze.co/187240083)Please note that the survey will close on***Tuesday, 3rd October***. We appreciate you taking the time to share your opinions. Please feel free to share any additional thoughts or concerns in the#social-mediachannel as well!
> 
> Thank you for your continued contributions to the Bioconductor community:heart:

*08:21:15 - @Frederick Tan has joined the channel*

## 2023-10-11

*13:20:24 - @Andrzej Białaś has joined the channel*

## 2023-11-17

**Maria Doyle** (04:38:10):
> :loudspeaker:Bioconductor is leaving Twitter/X. For more details, please read our blog post[https://blog.bioconductor.org/posts/2023-11-17-twitter-exit/](https://blog.bioconductor.org/posts/2023-11-17-twitter-exit/)
  - Attachment (blog.bioconductor.org): [Bioconductor community blog - Bioconductor is leaving Twitter/X](https://blog.bioconductor.org/posts/2023-11-17-twitter-exit/)
    > We’re transitioning away from Twitter/X in favor of platforms more aligned with our Code of Conduct.


## 2023-11-18

**Michael Love** (09:20:19):
> I think lots of people see the reasoning behind this decision.


## 2023-11-20

**Maria Doyle** (05:55:04):
> Thanks Mike! Appreciate the support.


## 2023-11-23

**Lluís Revilla** (09:38:45):
> In the past, Bioconductor's social media didn't acknowledge special occasions. Recently, I have observed some holiday-related posts. However, I note that these posts primarily celebrate USA holidays and not international ones.  I am uncertain if this is a deliberate strategy or my personal interpretation, but I wanted to share my thoughts to help clarify the intended audience.

**Kevin Rue-Albrecht** (09:48:13):
> Good point. I suspect this might be an unintentional bias towards events those preparing and posting those contents are familiar with. I wouldn't expect any one person to track down all the calendar dates important to all the communities represented in the Bioconductor project. A simple solution to this would be to crowdsource from the community the dates and occasions that the community would like to see featured in Bioconductor posts on social media. Hopefully, we won't run into any situation whereby an event celebrated by certain members of the community would be offending to other members of the community.

**Kevin Rue-Albrecht** (09:49:53):
> Instead, I would hope that inclusiveness would be reflected in the community in accepting that with one's own important dates being featured, one would welcome dates important to others may also be featured.

**Lori Shepherd** (12:41:50):
> Bioconductorcoremembers and official accounts as far as I'm aware do not post for social occasions or holidays; it might be just individuals posting? Can you point to some holiday related posts?

**Maria Doyle** (12:55:56):
> Lluís and Kevin, thank you for your feedback and comments. Lori, the holiday posts Lluís referred to were initiated by our social media intern, aiming to add a more personal touch to our presence online. We recognise that these posts were more U.S.-centric, which we now see as an oversight.
> 
> In line with Kevin’s suggestion, I’ll add this topic to the agenda for our next social media working group meeting -whether we continue with holiday posts and if so, whether we should crowdsource holiday dates from our community for more global inclusivity.

**Lluís Revilla** (12:58:04):
> Here are a few:
> * Thanksgiving[https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111459753052706484](https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111459753052706484)
> * Hallowen:[https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111331403758858738](https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111331403758858738),[https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111330703536624755](https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111330703536624755),[https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111329046457105716](https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111329046457105716)

**Lluís Revilla** (13:00:43):
> The quality of the images is high:smile:

**Lluís Revilla** (13:01:26):
> If the goal is to add a personal touch I would suggest to the working group that the account could  answer questions or redirecting users to the right people (maintainers, core members...) or places (Slack, website, support site...).

**Maria Doyle** (13:10:09):
> Thank you for noting the high quality of the images – it’s great to hear that their standard is appreciated!:smile:With our intern finishing in a few weeks, we will indeed face a challenge in continuing to produce custom-designed content at the same level. Crowdsourcing images and content could be one alternative. Alternatively, as you suggest, focusing more on interaction could be another effective approach.


## 2023-12-07

**Kevin Rue-Albrecht** (05:13:07):
> FYI: received from ISCB today
  - File (PNG): [IMG_3791](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F068W3J98MU/img_3791.png)


## 2023-12-10

**Michael Love** (13:28:50):
> Blue sky invite codes:
> bsky-social-pq42u-2chxp
> bsky-social-5pwxz-4pcwk


## 2023-12-19

*10:53:58 - @Svetlana Ugarcina Perovic has joined the channel*
**Svetlana Ugarcina Perovic** (10:57:21):
> Hello everyone, our Outreachy interns***Esther, Chioma and Peace just started to blog about their outreachy/bugsigdb experience.***I announced their posts on LinkedIn (cc-ed Bioconductor), X (our interns use X) and BlueSky. Feel free to share them via the Bioconductor Mastodon account.
> 
> Thanks and enjoy reading their inspiring stories![https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1736739617984880668](https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1736739617984880668)[https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1736933385879126150](https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1736933385879126150)[https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1737124514515841256](https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1737124514515841256)
  - Attachment (X (formerly Twitter)): [Svetlana U. Perović (@svetlana_up) on X](https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1736739617984880668)
    > Read a very first blog by Esther, a #BugSigDB intern:
    > "When we embrace kindness as a core value, we strive to be helpful and supportive, creating a positive and welcoming environment."
    > 
    > Much more to come from Esther and her #BugSigDB curations!
    > 
    > #proudmentor 
    > cc @LeviWaldron1
  - Attachment (X (formerly Twitter)): [Svetlana U. Perović (@svetlana_up) on X](https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1736933385879126150)
    > Chioma, an @outreachy intern, wrote about her core values, how she applied for internship and why did she choose the Bioconductor Microbiome Study Curation project. 
    > 
    > Stay tuned for more Chioma’s stories, tips & tricks on #BugSigDB-ing! 
    > 
    > cc @LeviWaldron1
  - Attachment (X (formerly Twitter)): [Svetlana U. Perović (@svetlana_up) on X](https://twitter.com/svetlana_up/status/1737124514515841256)
    > .@PeaceSandy3 just started her @outreachy journey (together with @oma_bekee and @folakunmiesther) of contribution and growth while curating microbiome studies
    > 
    > Read Peace's point of view on #BugSigDB-ing!
    > 
    > cc @LeviWaldron1
    > 
    > [https://t.co/gzHentAI60](https://t.co/gzHentAI60)

**Maria Doyle** (12:08:27):
> Thanks for notifying here@Svetlana Ugarcina PerovicI’ve reposted those to Bioconductor LinkedIn account and posted from Mastodon here[https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111608165082261048](https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111608165082261048)
  - Attachment (genomic.social): [Bioconductor (@bioconductor@genomic.social)](https://genomic.social/@bioconductor/111608165082261048)
    > Attached: 1 image
    > 
    > :star2: Welcome to our Outreachy interns! :star2:
    > 
    > We're excited to welcome Esther Afuape, Chioma Onyido and Peace Sandy as our newest interns at Bioconductor, where they'll be contributing to the BugSigDB project. Each has started blogging about their journey:
    > 
    > - Esther's blog: [https://kunmiwrites.wordpress.com](https://kunmiwrites.wordpress.com)
    > - Chioma's blog: [https://chiomaonyido.wordpress.com](https://chiomaonyido.wordpress.com)
    > - Peace's blog: [https://peacesandy.hashnode.dev/kicking-off-outreachy-week-one-highlights-a-journey-of-contribution-and-growth](https://peacesandy.hashnode.dev/kicking-off-outreachy-week-one-highlights-a-journey-of-contribution-and-growth)
    > 
    > We encourage you to follow their journey and support their growth! 
    > 
    > @outreachy@hachyderm.io #OpenSource #BugSigDB #BioconductorCommunity


## 2023-12-24

**Maria Doyle** (07:02:27) (in thread):
> Quick update: Our intern put together some posts for Christmas Eve and New Year’s before this discussion. We’ve decided to go ahead with them to appreciate her effort and keep things inclusive. We’ve tweaked them a bit to better reflect everyone in our community.


## 2024-01-11

**Svetlana Ugarcina Perovic** (04:02:08):
> Hello, NEW posts by our outreachy interns are out and shared with via LinkedIn, X and BlueSky[https://www.linkedin.com/posts/svetlana-ugarcina-perovic_bugsigdb-ing-chronicles-1-ac\[…\]486837637120-v7NP?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/svetlana-ugarcina-perovic_bugsigdb-ing-chronicles-1-activity-7150794486837637120-v7NP?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop)
  - Attachment (linkedin.com): [Svetlana Perovic on LinkedIn: BugSigDB-ing Chronicles 1](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/svetlana-ugarcina-perovic_bugsigdb-ing-chronicles-1-activity-7150794486837637120-v7NP?utm_source=share&amp;utm_medium=member_desktop)
    > NEW Outreachy #bugsigdb (Bioconductor) posts by Chioma Onyido
    > 
    > [https://lnkd.in/e-bbHDNN](https://lnkd.in/e-bbHDNN)
    > 
    > 1/2


## 2024-02-06

**Svetlana Ugarcina Perovic** (15:31:55):
> @Maria Doyle[https://www.theverge.com/2024/2/6/24062837/bluesky-drops-invite-system-begins-federation-at-protocol](https://www.theverge.com/2024/2/6/24062837/bluesky-drops-invite-system-begins-federation-at-protocol)
  - Attachment (The Verge): [Bluesky is ready to open up](https://www.theverge.com/2024/2/6/24062837/bluesky-drops-invite-system-begins-federation-at-protocol)
    > Is there room for another kind of decentralized social media?


## 2024-03-11

*10:15:27 - @Melysssa Minto has joined the channel*

## 2024-10-22

**Susan Holmes** (07:51:09):
> Hi@Maria Doyleand@Lori Shepherdand@Laurent Gattoand@Michael Loveothers from the past media group, could we revisit the presence of Biconductor on Bluesky which seems to have taken off as the alternative to twitter in the last month? I did put some announcements etc but I think that going forward it would be good for Bioconductor to have an account and a presence there as well as on mastodon.

**Michael Love** (07:55:03):
> As an anecdotal aside -- I've been trying out LinkedIn since July this year and it's pretty greatfor stats and bioinformatics. They have posting like these other platformsnow
  - File (PNG): [IMG_7815](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F07SW6VPDD3/img_7815.png)

**Michael Love** (07:56:10) (in thread):
> Another example
  - File (PNG): [IMG_7816](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F07SZ2WAWRY/img_7816.png)

**Michael Love** (07:58:33) (in thread):
> I've tried all the platforms now, and happily ignoring twitter/X since 1-2 years. People are scattered all over which is unfortunate but at least we have alternatives!

**Svetlana Ugarcina Perovic** (08:20:00) (in thread):
> Yes!

**Svetlana Ugarcina Perovic** (08:27:39):
> Just to add, MVIF ([https://www.microbiome-vif.org/en-US](https://www.microbiome-vif.org/en-US)) is present on X, BlueSky and LinkedIn. And we noticed the same as@Susan Holmesand@Michael Love


## 2024-10-24

**Maria Doyle** (15:33:56):
> Thanks, everyone! Buffer added Bluesky support back in July, and with the growing interest from the community (such as here), I’ve gone ahead and set it up as an additional platform for Bioconductor announcements (alongside LinkedIn and Mastodon). You can find us now here:[https://bsky.app/profile/bioconductor.bsky.social](https://bsky.app/profile/bioconductor.bsky.social). First post goes out tomorrow at ~11am ET for the GBCC2025 logo design contest:slightly_smiling_face:
  - Attachment (Bluesky Social): [Bioconductor (@bioconductor.bsky.social)](https://bsky.app/profile/bioconductor.bsky.social)
    > Bioconductor: Free, open-source software for rigorous, reproducible biological data analysis with R. Join our diverse community of developers and data scientists.
    > [https://bioconductor.org/](https://bioconductor.org/)


## 2024-10-29

*11:24:48 - @Kylie Bemis has joined the channel*

## 2024-11-05

*09:22:20 - @Vince Carey has joined the channel*

## 2024-11-16

**Federico Marini** (09:44:13):
> I did put some time in to collect the ones I already could see over there where the sky is blue -->[https://go.bsky.app/AFc6k3](https://go.bsky.app/AFc6k3)
  - Attachment (Bluesky Social): [R/Bioconductor developers & community](https://go.bsky.app/AFc6k3)
    > Join the conversation

**Federico Marini** (09:44:43):
> Those among us missing in there, just shoot out either here or there:wink:


## 2024-11-17

**Aedin Culhane** (06:11:10):
> Is it worth importing tweets (see[https://www.wikihow.com/Import-Twitter-to-Bluesky](https://www.wikihow.com/Import-Twitter-to-Bluesky))
  - Attachment (wikiHow): [How To Import Twitter To Bluesky: Follows and Tweets](https://www.wikihow.com/Import-Twitter-to-Bluesky)
    > Seamlessly move from Twitter to Bluesky with this handy guide If you're migrating from Twitter/X to Bluesky, the new platform can feel lonely until you find people to follow. Fortunately, there are tools available that make it easy to...


## 2024-11-18

**Federico Marini** (19:10:03):
> This one worked pretty smooth in my hands

**Federico Marini** (19:10:04):
> [https://www.sky-follower-bridge.dev/get-started.html](https://www.sky-follower-bridge.dev/get-started.html)
  - Attachment (sky-follower-bridge.dev): [Sky Follower Bridge](https://www.sky-follower-bridge.dev/get-started.html)
    > Instantly find and follow the same users from your 𝕏 followers on Bluesky

**Federico Marini** (19:10:27):
> I used the firefox extension, and it does work as it should, suggestions are mostly correct


## 2024-11-19

**Svetlana Ugarcina Perovic** (04:46:55) (in thread):
> Thanks for sharing this!

**Svetlana Ugarcina Perovic** (06:01:05) (in thread):
> [Chrome Web Store](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/sky-follower-bridge/behhbpbpmailcnfbjagknjngnfdojpko)works smoothly!
  - Attachment (chromewebstore.google.com): [Sky Follower Bridge - Chrome Web Store](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/sky-follower-bridge/behhbpbpmailcnfbjagknjngnfdojpko)
    > Instantly find and follow the same users from your Twitter follows on Bluesky.


## 2025-03-14

**Michael Love** (18:06:32):
  - File (PNG): [Screenshot 2025-03-14 at 6.04.38 PM.png](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F08HVCJN4KU/screenshot_2025-03-14_at_6.04.38___pm.png)
